methodology.description: Shared CSV fixtures; data loaded once; only model fit timed; Julia JIT warmed up before samples.
methodology.note: Single-factor random-slopes fast path (SingleFactorSlopesCache in src/math.rs): block-diagonal ΛᵀZᵀZΛ assembly + reused sparse LDL on the full q×q system; deviance-only θ evaluations. Measured with uncommitted slopes cache atop git_sha. Prior sleepstudy_reml cold median was ~2.7 ms Rust vs ~0.77 ms Julia (~3.5×) on the 2026-07-06 reference.

comparisons.notes: First tier-A re-benchmark after random-slopes block LDL path; Rust faster than Julia on cold lmer() and fit_prepared. Down from ~3.5× Julia (2026-07-06 table).

summary.interpretation: sleepstudy_reml (canonical random-slopes LMM) is now within the 1.5× cold-fit bar and faster than MixedModels.jl on this workstation (~0.8× cold lmer(), ~0.74× fit_prepared vs Julia fit). Synthetic tier-A status unchanged in fair-rust-julia-reference-2026-07-09.json.
